name: Sports and Action Photography Guide - Master the techniques

step.name: Research and plan before you arrive
step.text: Know the storyline. Identify key players, milestones, kit colours, sun direction at kick-off. Confirm photographer access policy and consent arrangements if shooting children.

step.name: Arrive early, watch the venue, choose your position
step.text: Arrive 30 minutes before kick-off. Walk the venue. Identify where action will concentrate, sun direction, cleanest backgrounds. Pick a primary and backup shooting position.

step.name: Set up your camera before play starts
step.text: Continuous AF, single centre point, burst mode high. Shutter speed at table value, aperture wide, ISO managed. Test on warm-up.

step.name: Watch the play, anticipate the moments
step.text: Camera at chest height, both eyes on play. Bring camera up only when you know where next action will be. Read patterns and player tendencies.

step.name: Capture in short deliberate bursts
step.text: 3-6 frame bursts at moments of action. Mix freezes with the occasional pan. Avoid holding the shutter down for long sprays.

step.name: Adjust as conditions change
step.text: Check exposure every 10-15 minutes. Adjust shutter, aperture, or ISO as light shifts. Raise ISO before letting shutter speed drop.

step.name: Cull and edit with discipline
step.text: Import, rate, cull aggressively. Keep 5-10% of frames as keepers. Edit only those. Output sized for the destination — print, web, or messaging.
